title: Public Records Request — Denver Generative AI Records

communications.subject: Colorado Open Records Act Request: Public Records Request — Denver Generative AI Records
communications.communication:
Subject: Public Records Request — AI Chatbot Interaction Logs

April 9, 2026

Records Administrator
City and County of Denver
201 W. Colfax Avenue
Denver, CO 80202

Dear Records Administrator,

I am writing on behalf of a team of researchers and journalists from Stanford University, the University of Southern California, KNKX Public Radio and the New York Times. We are studying how government agencies are adopting and using AI tools in their daily work.

We are writing to request records related to the City and County of Denver's use of generative AI tools by city employees, including Microsoft Copilot Chat and Ask Dee (AiseraGPT).

Pursuant to the Colorado Open Records Act, C.R.S. § 24-72-203(3)(b), we are requesting the following records:

1. All interaction logs from Ask Dee (AiseraGPT), Microsoft Copilot Chat, Sunny AI, ChatGPT, Google Gemini, Claude, or any other generative AI chatbot tool — including prompts submitted by City and County of Denver employees and responses generated by the AI — for the period from January 1, 2025 through the date this request is processed. We request these records in their native electronic format where available (e.g., HTML exports, JSON, or CSV).

2. Any policies, guidelines, acceptable use policies, or training materials governing City and County of Denver employee use of Ask Dee, Microsoft Copilot Chat, Sunny AI, or other generative AI chatbot tools.

3. Any data retention or deletion policies applicable to AI chatbot interaction logs, including any schedules for automatic deletion of interaction data.

4. Administrative usage statistics showing number of users, sessions, and queries processed for any generative AI tools deployed to city employees.

We are seeking the substantive content of AI interactions for research purposes. We do not need personally identifying information of individual employees (such as names and email addresses).

The City and County of Denver has deployed Ask Dee, powered by AiseraGPT, which uses domain-specific large language models to automatically resolve employee IT support requests such as software provisioning, account troubleshooting, and hardware issues, auto-resolving 81% of inquiries (see Aisera, "City & County of Denver Customer Story," https://aisera.com/customers/city-and-county-of-denver/). Denver's Chief AI and Information Officer Suma Nallapati has overseen the development of the Sunny AI platform and the city's partnership with Microsoft on Copilot Chat for employee use (see Harvard Data-Smart City Solutions, "Personalizing Government at Scale: Denver's AI Strategy," https://datasmart.hks.harvard.edu/personalizing-denver-ai; see also GovTech, "How a New Title for Denver's CIO Helps Power AI Work," https://www.govtech.com/artificial-intelligence/how-a-new-title-for-denvers-cio-helps-power-ai-work). Denver has also issued an RFP seeking skilled AI vendors to drive innovation and improve the resident experience (see City and County of Denver, "AI RFP Press Release," https://denvergov.org/Government/Agencies-Departments-Offices/Agencies-Departments-Offices-Directory/Technology-Services/News/AI-RFP-Press-Release).

We note that records of this type have been made available in response to public records requests in other jurisdictions. See Cascade PBS, WA City Officials Are Using ChatGPT to Write Government Documents (Aug. 2025), https://www.cascadepbs.org/news/2025/08/wa-city-officials-are-using-chatgpt-to-write-government-documents/; see also Texas State AI Usage — Texas Department of Transportation (TxDOT), MuckRock, https://www.muckrock.com/foi/texas-109/texas-state-ai-usage-texas-department-of-transportation-txdot-196227/#files.

The results of this research will be published in peer-reviewed academic venues and will contribute to public understanding of how public servants interact with AI systems. This request is made by an academic research institution for scholarly, non-commercial purposes; as such, we request a fee waiver or reduction. If a fee waiver is not granted, please inform me of estimated costs before proceeding. I am willing to pay fees up to $20.

I understand that this may be a novel type of records request and I am happy to answer any technical questions. Please feel free to contact me at the email address below.

Thank you for your time and assistance.

Sincerely,
Alexander Spangher
Postdoctoral Researcher
Stanford University
alexspan@stanford.edu

P.S. To facilitate your response, we note that Ask Dee (AiseraGPT) maintains server-side conversation logs and resolution data accessible through the Aisera administrative dashboard. For Microsoft Copilot Chat, interaction histories may be available through the Microsoft 365 admin console or the Purview compliance center. For ChatGPT, conversation histories can be exported by individual users via Settings > Data Controls > Export Data. We are happy to provide additional technical guidance if that would be helpful.

P.P.S. Colorado has enacted one of the nation's most comprehensive AI records mandates. The Colorado AI Act (SB 24-205, signed 2024, effective February 1, 2026) requires deployers of high-risk AI systems — including government agencies — to implement risk management policies, conduct impact assessments, and maintain documentation and records of AI system use and outcomes. C.R.S. § 6-1-1701 et seq. The Colorado Open Records Act defines "public records" to include all writings made or maintained by any state agency regardless of format. C.R.S. § 24-72-202. Consistent with this trend nationally, NARA has made clear that chats and other electronic messages used for agency business are likely government records. NARA Bulletin 2023-02 (Jan. 5, 2023), https://www.archives.gov/records-mgmt/bulletins/2023/2023-02.

communications.subject: RE: Colorado Open Records Act Request: Public Records Request — Denver Generative AI Records
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

I wanted to follow up on the following Colorado Open Records Act request, copied below, and originally submitted on April 16, 2026. Please let me know when I can expect to receive a response.

Thanks for your help, and let me know if further clarification is needed.

communications.subject: RE: [EXTERNAL] RE: Colorado Open Records Act Request: Public Records Request — Denver Generative AI Records
communications.communication:
Hello,

All CORA requests are processed according to a uniform fee schedule. The City and County of Denver (more specifically Technology Services (TS)) does not grant fee waivers regardless of the requesting entity or the intended use of the records post-release.

There are approximately 7,300 potentially responsive records based on your search criteria. These records must be reviewed for the presence of protected and exempt information. At an estimated 6 minutes of review per record, this request will require 730 man-hours.

Following the use of the statutory free hour for searching and compiling this estimate, the remaining work is billed at the allowed rate of $41.37 per hour.

Estimated Cost Breakdown:

  *   Review Time: 7,300 records x 6 minutes = 43,800 minutes
  *   Total Hours: 43,800 minutes / 60 = 730 hours
  *   Total Cost: 730 hours × $41.37/hr = $30,200.10

Please let me know how you would like to proceed with this request. The City requires payment in full prior to starting any pulling of records for review for redaction and release.

Tyler Z




[cid:image001.png@01DCD189.30562930]
311<https://www.denvergov.org/content/denvergov/en/denver-311-help-center.html> | denvergov.org<http://www.denvergov.org/> | Denver 8 TV<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/denver8.tv/__;!!M87Ej6RJKlw!S0MSgK_mQ_yI-dSgxeSmHlWf_FW46ffEFbqAn7d0g99II2IQkqJxMZv3WLFS7jou78upRCfDEdc0gO2Tfdpn7i0$> | Facebook<https://urldefense.com/v3/__http:/www.facebook.com/cityandcountyofdenver__;!!M87Ej6RJKlw!S0MSgK_mQ_yI-dSgxeSmHlWf_FW46ffEFbqAn7d0g99II2IQkqJxMZv3WLFS7jou78upRCfDEdc0gO2TQwEc05c$> | Twitter<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/twitter.com/CityofDenver__;!!M87Ej6RJKlw!S0MSgK_mQ_yI-dSgxeSmHlWf_FW46ffEFbqAn7d0g99II2IQkqJxMZv3WLFS7jou78upRCfDEdc0gO2T11-OSJw$> | Instagram<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/www.instagram.com/therealcityofdenver/__;!!M87Ej6RJKlw!S0MSgK_mQ_yI-dSgxeSmHlWf_FW46ffEFbqAn7d0g99II2IQkqJxMZv3WLFS7jou78upRCfDEdc0gO2T7H0c4ig$>

communications.subject: RE: Colorado Open Records Act Request: Public Records Request — Denver Generative AI Records
communications.communication:
Dear Denver Technology Services CORA Office,

  Thank you for your response. I understand that Denver charges a uniform fee for CORA
  requests. However, I would like to formally request a fee reduction pursuant to C.R.S. §
  24-72-205(4), which authorizes custodians to reduce or waive fees when electronic records
  are to be used for a public purpose, "including public agency program support, nonprofit
  activities, journalism, and academic research." This request is made by Stanford University for scholarly, non-commercial, peer-reviewed
  research. I believe this qualifies under the statute's public-purpose provision.

  Regarding the 7,300 potentially responsive records: could you clarify whether these logs are
   available as a bulk export from the Microsoft 365 admin console or Purview compliance
  center? I'm just thinking that maybe a bulk export would require less staff time for search and retrieval,
  which should substantially reduce the cost.

  Thank you,
  Alex

communications.subject: RE: [EXTERNAL] RE: Colorado Open Records Act Request: Public Records Request — Denver Generative AI Records
communications.communication:
Alex,

All CORA requests, including those submitted for academic or journalistic purposes, are subject to the same uniform fee schedule. Technology Services does not grant fee waivers or reductions based on the entity or the intended use of the records.

Regarding the retrieval process: The method of export does not change the requirement for the City to perform a thorough line-by-line review of each potentially responsive record.   As stated previously, these potentially responsive records must be manually reviewed and redacted for protected, exempt, and privileged information.

The bulk of the estimated cost is attributed to mandatory review labor, not the technical act of the search (as the search and our communication has exhausted the statuatory free hour) or export. The estimate of $30,200.10 remains the requirement to proceed.

Please let us know if you would like to provide payment in full to begin the review process,

Tyler Z


[cid:image001.png@01DCD24A.88A7BF60]
311<https://www.denvergov.org/content/denvergov/en/denver-311-help-center.html> | denvergov.org<http://www.denvergov.org/> | Denver 8 TV<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/denver8.tv/__;!!M87Ej6RJKlw!S0MSgK_mQ_yI-dSgxeSmHlWf_FW46ffEFbqAn7d0g99II2IQkqJxMZv3WLFS7jou78upRCfDEdc0gO2Tfdpn7i0$> | Facebook<https://urldefense.com/v3/__http:/www.facebook.com/cityandcountyofdenver__;!!M87Ej6RJKlw!S0MSgK_mQ_yI-dSgxeSmHlWf_FW46ffEFbqAn7d0g99II2IQkqJxMZv3WLFS7jou78upRCfDEdc0gO2TQwEc05c$> | Twitter<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/twitter.com/CityofDenver__;!!M87Ej6RJKlw!S0MSgK_mQ_yI-dSgxeSmHlWf_FW46ffEFbqAn7d0g99II2IQkqJxMZv3WLFS7jou78upRCfDEdc0gO2T11-OSJw$> | Instagram<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/www.instagram.com/therealcityofdenver/__;!!M87Ej6RJKlw!S0MSgK_mQ_yI-dSgxeSmHlWf_FW46ffEFbqAn7d0g99II2IQkqJxMZv3WLFS7jou78upRCfDEdc0gO2T7H0c4ig$>

communications.subject: RE: Colorado Open Records Act Request: Public Records Request — Denver Generative AI Records
communications.communication:
Hi Tyler,

Thank you for your response. Perhaps it would be good to hop on a phone call so I can better explain our research purposes and discuss a mutually agreed-upon narrowing of the request that could meet these purposes?

  1. I am willing to substantially narrow my request to a subset of employees and agencies but want to make sure we can come to a good faith determination of which employees/agencies this would entail, that could serve our purposes as well as not burden your office/staff?

  2. I do have some questions around fees and I would appreciate some clarity. Gemini interaction logs are "computer output other than word processing" under C.R.S. § 24-72-205(4), which limits fees to "actual incremental costs of providing the electronic services and products."  I understand, clearly, the need to review and redact, and I don't want to impose undue burdens on your staff. But I would like to understand better which subsection you are applying and why the "actual incremental costs" standard in subsection (4) does not govern this request.

Let me know when works for you and what a good phone number would be to reach you. Thank you so much again for your time, public service and engagement.

Thank you,
Alex

communications.subject: RE: [EXTERNAL] RE: Colorado Open Records Act Request: Public Records Request — Denver Generative AI Records
communications.communication:
Alex,

I appreciate your willingness to jump on a call. However, I would prefer to keep the conversation going through your chosen venue to keep the transparency of this thread whole and complete.

The cost associated with this request is not for incremental costs associated with C.R.S. § 24-72-205(4). The costs are strictly surrounding labor and staff time for the entire process as per C.R.S. § 24-72-205(6); I apologize if that was not explicitly clear in the original estimate.

You are welcome to convey a narrowed scope in this email thread, and I will provide you with an updated estimate.

Tyler Z



[cid:image001.png@01DCD2FD.F68F0840]
311<https://www.denvergov.org/content/denvergov/en/denver-311-help-center.html> | denvergov.org<http://www.denvergov.org/> | Denver 8 TV<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/denver8.tv/__;!!M87Ej6RJKlw!S0MSgK_mQ_yI-dSgxeSmHlWf_FW46ffEFbqAn7d0g99II2IQkqJxMZv3WLFS7jou78upRCfDEdc0gO2Tfdpn7i0$> | Facebook<https://urldefense.com/v3/__http:/www.facebook.com/cityandcountyofdenver__;!!M87Ej6RJKlw!S0MSgK_mQ_yI-dSgxeSmHlWf_FW46ffEFbqAn7d0g99II2IQkqJxMZv3WLFS7jou78upRCfDEdc0gO2TQwEc05c$> | Twitter<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/twitter.com/CityofDenver__;!!M87Ej6RJKlw!S0MSgK_mQ_yI-dSgxeSmHlWf_FW46ffEFbqAn7d0g99II2IQkqJxMZv3WLFS7jou78upRCfDEdc0gO2T11-OSJw$> | Instagram<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/www.instagram.com/therealcityofdenver/__;!!M87Ej6RJKlw!S0MSgK_mQ_yI-dSgxeSmHlWf_FW46ffEFbqAn7d0g99II2IQkqJxMZv3WLFS7jou78upRCfDEdc0gO2T7H0c4ig$>

communications.subject: RE: Colorado Open Records Act Request: Public Records Request — Denver Generative AI Records
communications.communication:
Thanks Tyler,

The 7.3k records you have identified have been generated by how much individuals? I am willing to narrow the scope of my request to 1k records evenly split between individuals in the following roles: Policy/regulatory, Communications/public affairs, Program/project management, Administrative/executive support, Budget/finance, Senior leadership.

I'm wondering if, from there, we can come to a good faith compromise that does not burden your office and is reasonable for us. To be honest, even a 10% reduction in the original $30k cost is far out of budget for us as an academic lab. The data we are requesting is coming from a place of public spirit. For some background, we are seeking specifically to understand (1) how government employees are using generative AI tools, (2) how these tools might help government (3) ways in which they can be improved to better meet the needs of federal workers.

communications.subject: RE: Colorado Open Records Act Request: Public Records Request — Denver Generative AI Records
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

I wanted to follow up on the following Colorado Open Records Act request, copied below, and originally submitted on April 16, 2026. Please let me know when I can expect to receive a response.

Thanks for your help, and let me know if further clarification is needed.

communications.subject: RE: [EXTERNAL] RE: Colorado Open Records Act Request: Public Records Request — Denver Generative AI Records
communications.communication:
I will provide a set of viable options for your review by End of Day on Monday, 5/11/26. My goal is to identify a path that satisfies your research needs while minimizing labor hours and costs for both parties.

Regarding your proposal for 1,000 records categorized by specific roles, this is currently not a viable option. The metadata fields required to automate that breakdown do not exist in our systems; fulfilling this would require the creation of a new record. While the City may elect to perform such work under CORA, please be advised that custom data manipulation is billed at our standard professional service rates rather than the statute-defined search/retrieval rate should we elect to perform such work.

Please let me know if there are any other immediate items; otherwise, I look forward to re-engaging on Monday.


Tyler Z

[cid:image001.png@01DCDEFC.D3F47350]

communications.subject: RE: Colorado Open Records Act Request: Public Records Request — Denver Generative AI Records
communications.communication: Thank you so much for the response, Tyler. Please let me know if there are ways I can restrict the 1,000 records that would be inline with your metadata and easily acheivable.

communications.subject: RE: [EXTERNAL] RE: Colorado Open Records Act Request: Public Records Request — Denver Generative AI Records
communications.communication:
Good morning,

I am currently extracting the 1,000 most recent records from the system. We are also reviewing event logs for metadata and other exportable items that may align with your study’s data points. As previously noted, our systems do not inherently tie positions to users, so this will be a raw data pull of the most recent activity.

We encountered a technical issue with the solution’s admin panel during the export process and are currently engaged with the vendor for a resolution. I expect to have this data compiled and released to you by the end of the week.

Please let me know if you have any immediate questions; otherwise, I will follow up once the vendor fix is implemented.



[cid:image001.png@01DCE156.001D4EB0]
311<https://www.denvergov.org/content/denvergov/en/denver-311-help-center.html> | denvergov.org<http://www.denvergov.org/> | Denver 8 TV<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/denver8.tv/__;!!M87Ej6RJKlw!S0MSgK_mQ_yI-dSgxeSmHlWf_FW46ffEFbqAn7d0g99II2IQkqJxMZv3WLFS7jou78upRCfDEdc0gO2Tfdpn7i0$> | Facebook<https://urldefense.com/v3/__http:/www.facebook.com/cityandcountyofdenver__;!!M87Ej6RJKlw!S0MSgK_mQ_yI-dSgxeSmHlWf_FW46ffEFbqAn7d0g99II2IQkqJxMZv3WLFS7jou78upRCfDEdc0gO2TQwEc05c$> | Twitter<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/twitter.com/CityofDenver__;!!M87Ej6RJKlw!S0MSgK_mQ_yI-dSgxeSmHlWf_FW46ffEFbqAn7d0g99II2IQkqJxMZv3WLFS7jou78upRCfDEdc0gO2T11-OSJw$> | Instagram<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/www.instagram.com/therealcityofdenver/__;!!M87Ej6RJKlw!S0MSgK_mQ_yI-dSgxeSmHlWf_FW46ffEFbqAn7d0g99II2IQkqJxMZv3WLFS7jou78upRCfDEdc0gO2T7H0c4ig$>

communications.subject: RE: Colorado Open Records Act Request: Public Records Request — Denver Generative AI Records
communications.communication: Thank you so much, Tyler!

communications.subject: RE: [EXTERNAL] RE: Colorado Open Records Act Request: Public Records Request — Denver Generative AI Records
communications.communication:
Good Evening,

I have attached what I am able to easily scrape together. The first attachement named Aisera requests you will find what our CCD employees have asked our AI IT chagbot. Unfortunately, the system does not offer the ability to export names of who wrote what in the current build.

For the PP Event Monitor 90 days file. That is our event monirtor that captures what it can when staff use the systems.This will probably be more useful for your study I was able to export and review 5K records of the last 90 days.  We are on G3 licensing for Microsoft, this does not allow for us to get prompts or responses for copilot chats in current build. I have also attached our AI policy.

I have only redacted the endpoint names for security purposes. Otherwise, I certify that these records are complete and final containing all information and data.

Tyler Z



[cid:image001.png@01DCE6EF.08E72310]

title: March 11 DA

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: March 11 DA
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Please see enclosed PDF

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Maxine Doogan

title: FOIA Request: 625 ILCS 5/2-130

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: FOIA Request: 625 ILCS 5/2-130
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

According to 625 ILCS 5/2-130 (https://www.ilga.gov/legislation/ilcs/fulltext.asp?DocName=062500050K2-130):

"An ALPR user shall not sell, share, allow access to, or transfer ALPR information to any state or local jurisdiction for the purpose of investigating or enforcing a law that:
        (1) denies or interferes with a person's right to choose or obtain reproductive health care services or any lawful health care services as defined by the Lawful Health Care Activity Act; or
        (2) permits the detention or investigation of a person based on the person's immigration status.
(c) Any ALPR user in this State, including any law enforcement agency of this State that uses ALPR systems, shall not share ALPR information with an out-of-state law enforcement agency without first obtaining a written declaration from the out-of-state law enforcement agency that it expressly affirms that ALPR information obtained shall not be used in a manner that violates subsection (b). If a written declaration of affirmation is not executed, the law enforcement agency shall not share the ALPR information with the out-of-state law enforcement agency."

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

The documents I am seeking include, but are not limited to:

1) Policies and Procedures: Any current policies and procedures that have been adopted to ensure compliance with 625 ILCS 5/2-130.

2) Training Materials: Any training materials provided to staff about 625 ILCS 5/2-130.

3) Written Declarations: Written declarations received from out-of-state law enforcement agencies that it expressly affirms that ALPR information obtained shall not be used in a manner that violates subsection (b) of 625 ILCS 5/2-130.

4) Audit or Compliance Reports: Any recent audit or compliance reports that assess the adherence to 625 ILCS 5/2-130.

5) Records of Violations: Records or reports of any violations of 625 ILCS 5/2-130 legislation, including unauthorized sharing of data.

6) Disciplinary Actions: Documentation of any disciplinary actions taken against individuals or departments for non-compliance with 625 ILCS 5/2-130.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

RTL

communications.subject: RE: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: FOIA Request: 625 ILCS 5/2-130
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

In Illinois, public bodies must respond to a FOIA request within 5 business days of receipt, with
possible extensions for specific reasons.
Initial Response Timeline: 5 Business Days: Upon receiving a FOIA request, a public body is required to respond within 5 business days.
This period starts the first business day after the request is received, not the day it is received.
You will get a response from us by May 7.

communications.communication:
Hello,

I am in receipt of your Illinois Freedom of Information Act (5 ILCS 140/1 et. seq.) request received by the Village of Forest on April 30, 2026, for the following records:

  *   Policies and Procedures: Any current policies and procedures that have been adopted to ensure compliance with 625 ILCS 5/2-130
  *   Training Materials: Any training materials provided to staff about 625 ILCS 5/2-130
  *   Written Declarations: Written declarations received from out-of-state law enforcement agencies that it expressly affirms that ALPR information obtained shall not be used in a manner that violates subsection (b) of 625 ILCS 5/2-130
  *   Audit or Compliance Reports: Any recent audit or compliance reports that assess the adherence to 625 ILCS 5/2-130
  *   Records of Violations: Records or reports of any violations of 625 ILCS 5/2-130 legislation, including unauthorized sharing of data
  *   Disciplinary Actions: Documentation of any disciplinary actions taken against individuals or departments for non-compliance with 625 ILCS 5/2-130

Your request is partially granted. See below:


  *   Policies and Procedures: Any current policies and procedures that have been adopted to ensure compliance with 625 ILCS 5/2-130. -- Attached
  *   Training Materials: Any training materials provided to staff about 625 ILCS 5/2-130. - Refer to attached policy, which is based upon IL statute
  *   Written Declarations: Written declarations received from out-of-state law enforcement agencies that it expressly affirms that ALPR information obtained shall not be used in a manner that violates subsection (b) of 625 ILCS 5/2-130. - Forest Park PD only shares with Illinois Agencies
  *   Audit or Compliance Reports: Any recent audit or compliance reports that assess the adherence to 625 ILCS 5/2-130.  - Not required by statute.
  *   Records of Violations: Records or reports of any violations of 625 ILCS 5/2-130 legislation, including unauthorized sharing of data. - None on file
  *   Disciplinary Actions: Documentation of any disciplinary actions taken against individuals or departments for non-compliance with 625 ILCS 5/2-130. - None on file


You have a right to have the partial denial of your request reviewed by the Public Access Counselor, (PAC) at the Office of the Illinois Attorney General.  5 ILCS 140/9.5(a).  You can file your Request for Review with the PAC by writing to:
Public Access Counselor
Office of the Illinois Attorney General
500 South 2nd Street
Springfield, IL 62701
PAC hotline: 877-299-3642
public.access@ilag.gov<mailto:public.access@ilag.gov>

FOIA Officer
517 Desplaines Avenue
Forest Park, IL  60130
(708) 615-6211
 [cid:image001.jpg@01DCDE0D.D8C6A370]

This communication from the Village of Forest Park constitutes an electronic communication within the meaning of the Electronic Communications Privacy Act, 18 USC 2510-2515, and its disclosure is strictly limited to the recipient intended by the sender of this message. This communication is for the sole use of the intended recipient and receipt by anyone other than the intended recipient does not constitute loss of the confidential or privileged nature of the communication. Any review or distribution by others is strictly prohibited.

- RTL

title: Public Records Request — Connecticut Generative AI Records

communications.subject: Connecticut Freedom of Information Act Request: Public Records Request — Connecticut Generative AI Records
communications.communication:
Subject: Public Records Request — AI Chatbot Interaction Logs

April 9, 2026

Records Officer
Bureau of Information Technology Solutions
Connecticut Department of Administrative Services
450 Columbus Boulevard
Hartford, CT 06103

Dear Records Officer,

I am writing on behalf of a team of researchers and journalists from Stanford University, the University of Southern California, KNKX Public Radio and the New York Times. We are studying how government agencies are adopting and using AI tools in their daily work.

We are writing to request records related to Connecticut's use of generative AI tools by state employees, including ChatGPT and Microsoft Copilot.

We are requesting the following records under Conn. Gen. Stat. § 1-206(a):

1. All interaction logs from ChatGPT, Microsoft Copilot, Google Gemini, Claude, or any other generative AI chatbot tool — including prompts submitted by state employees and responses generated by the AI — for the period from January 1, 2025 through the date this request is processed. We request these records in their native electronic format where available (e.g., HTML exports, JSON, or CSV).

2. Any policies, guidelines, acceptable use policies, or training materials governing state employee use of ChatGPT, Microsoft Copilot, or other generative AI chatbot tools.

3. Any data retention or deletion policies applicable to AI chatbot interaction logs, including any schedules for automatic deletion of interaction data.

4. Administrative usage statistics for ChatGPT, Microsoft Copilot, or other generative AI tools showing number of users, sessions, and queries processed.

We are seeking the substantive content of AI interactions for research purposes. We do not need personally identifying information of individual employees (such as names and email addresses).

Connecticut has conducted both a ChatGPT pilot and a Microsoft M365 Copilot trial, with over 260 employees taking specialized public sector AI training and engaging in experiential learning through these tools (see NASCIO Awards Submission, "State of Connecticut: Bureau of Information Technology," https://www.nascio.org/wp-content/uploads/2025/09/CT_Artificial-Intelligence.pdf). The state has also established an AI Enablement Lab for safe experimentation with AI technologies and maintains an annual AI inventory required by Public Act 23-16 (see Connecticut 2025 State AI Inventory, https://portal.ct.gov/das/knowledge-base/articles/bits/ai-inventory). Connecticut's Responsible AI Framework, released February 2024, provides governance guidelines for AI deployment across state agencies (see Connecticut Responsible AI Policy Framework, https://portal.ct.gov/-/media/OPM/Fin-General/Policies/CT-Responsible-AI-Policy-Framework-Final-02012024.pdf).

We note that records of this type have been made available in response to public records requests in other jurisdictions. See Cascade PBS, "WA City Officials Are Using ChatGPT to Write Government Documents" (Aug. 2025), https://www.cascadepbs.org/news/2025/08/wa-city-officials-are-using-chatgpt-to-write-government-documents/; see also Texas State AI Usage — Texas Department of Transportation (TxDOT), MuckRock, https://www.muckrock.com/foi/texas-109/texas-state-ai-usage-texas-department-of-transportation-txdot-196227/#files.

The results of this research will be published in peer-reviewed academic venues and will contribute to public understanding of how public servants interact with AI systems. This request is made by an academic research institution for scholarly, non-commercial purposes; as such, we request a fee waiver or reduction. If a fee waiver is not granted, please inform me of estimated costs before proceeding. I am willing to pay fees up to $20.

I understand that this may be a novel type of records request and I am happy to answer any technical questions. Please feel free to contact me at the email address below.

Thank you for your time and assistance.

Sincerely,
Alexander Spangher
Postdoctoral Researcher
Stanford University
alexspan@stanford.edu

P.S. To facilitate your response, we note that ChatGPT conversation histories can be exported by individual users via Settings > Data Controls > Export Data. For Microsoft Copilot, interaction histories may be available through the Microsoft 365 admin console or the Purview compliance center. For Google Gemini, interaction data may be available through Google Vault or Google Takeout. We are happy to provide additional technical guidance if that would be helpful.

P.P.S. Connecticut's Freedom of Information Act defines "public records" as any recorded data or information relating to the conduct of the public's business, including electronic data. Conn. Gen. Stat. § 1-200(5). Public Act 24-130 (2024) requires state agencies to complete an inventory of all AI systems used in consequential decision-making, including documentation of purpose, data inputs, and outcomes. Governor Lamont's Executive Order No. 1 (2023) directed the development of an AI policy framework emphasizing transparency and accountability. Consistent with this trend nationally, NARA has made clear that chats and other electronic messages used for agency business are likely government records. NARA Bulletin 2023-02 (Jan. 5, 2023), https://www.archives.gov/records-mgmt/bulletins/2023/2023-02.

communications.subject: [Records Center] FOI Records Request :: R000212-041626
communications.communication:
RE: FOI Records Request created on April 16, 2026, Reference # R000212-041626 
 
Dear Alexander , 
 
This message is to acknowledge receipt of your Freedom of Information request submitted on April 16, 2026 by the Department of Administrative Services. 
 
This letter does not constitute a denial of your request. Rather, in accordance with the Freedom of Information Act, we are in the process of researching and gathering documentation which may be responsive to it. Once the documents are gathered, we will then conduct a review to determine if statutory exemptions to disclosure may apply to some or all of the documents. Upon completion of that review, we will be able to provide you with any responsive, non-exempt documents. 
 
Please note the department reserves the right to charge any such other fees as may be appropriate pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act. 
 
Sincerely, 
 
DAS

communications.subject: [Records Center] FOI Records Request :: R000212-041626
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 

RE: FOI Records Request of April 16, 2026, Reference # R000212-041626. 
Dear Alexander Spangher, 
We are writing in response to Freedom of Information Act Request # R000212-041626.  We have reviewed our files and determined that our agency has responsive records.  Those records have been uploaded to the GovQA portal and can be accessed here: FOI Records Request - R000212-041626 
Please note that records for item 1 are being withheld as exempt from disclosure pursuant to the applicable statutes and/or privileges: 
C.G.S. 1-210(b)(1)  Preliminary drafts or notes provided the public agency has determined that the public interest in withholding such documents clearly outweighs the public interest in disclosure.

title: Bodycam Footage - Lawnmower Warrant Arrest - July 11, 2022 - Crestview FL

communications.subject: Florida Sunshine Law Request: Bodycam Footage - Lawnmower Warrant Arrest - July 11, 2022 - Crestview FL
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

All body-worn camera footage from an incident occurring on July 11, 2022 in Crestview, Florida, involving Okaloosa County Sheriff's deputies responding to serve a warrant on a male subject who attempted to flee on a lawnmower and was subsequently subdued with a Taser.
I would prefer the records fulfilled electronically via email or download link. In the event that fees are required, please notify me of the estimated cost before fulfilling the request.
Thank you for your assistance.
David Rennich

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

David Rennich

title: Information and Documents Related to 9/1/21 Suicide at Wyatt Detention Facility

communications.subject: Rhode Island Access to Public Records Act Request: Information and Documents Related to 9/1/21 Suicide at Wyatt Detention Facility
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Rhode Island Access to Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

In response to a February 2026 APRA request titled “Suicide attempts, self-harm incidents and deaths,” on March 20, 2026 the Central Falls Detention Facility Corporation released a one-page document with information about eight suicide attempts that took take place at the Wyatt between 2019 and the present.

One entry on that document, dated “9/1/2021,” reads:

“Suicide - At approx. 6:18am, Code white was initiated by Officer. He reported that Dt. Had hung himself. Control dispatched responders and medical to the scene. CPR was given a the CF rescue was called and the detainee was taken to RI Hospital where he was pronounced dead.”

I am requesting any and all available information and documents related to this incident (which appears to not previously have been publicly reported).

This includes:

-Transcript and recording (if available) of any 911 calls made to CFPD about the incident
-Any CFPD police reports about the incident
-Any emails among between Wyatt or CFDFC and CFPD about the incident
-Any records or reports from CF first responders about the incident

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Philip Eil

communications.subject: RE: Rhode Island Access to Public Records Act Request: Information and Documents Related to 9/1/21 Suicide at Wyatt Detention Facility
communications.communication:

communications.communication:
May 18, 2026

Mr. Philip Eil
MuckRock News
DEPT MR 209741
263 Huntington Avenue
Boston, MA 02115

VIA EMAIL
209741-90840717@requests.muckrock.com
RE:  ACCESS TO PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST
Dear Mr. Eil:
This Department’s review of your April 29, 2026, Access to Public Records Act (“APRA”) request is complete.  Specifically, you seek the following:
 “In response to a February 2026 APRA request titled "Suicide attempts, self-harm incidents and deaths:, on March 20, 2026 the Central Falls Detention Facility Corporation released a one-page document with information about eight suicide attempts that took place at the Wyatt between 2019 and the present.  One entry on that document, dated "9/1/21", reads:  "Suicide - At approx. 6:18am, Code white was initiated by Officer.  He reported that Dt. Had hung himself.  Control dispatched responders and medical to the scene.  CPR was given a the CF rescue was called and the detainee was taken to RI Hospital where he was pronounced dead."  I am requesting any and all available information and documents related to this incident (which appears to not previously have been publicly reported). This includes: Transcript and recording (if available) of any 911 calls made to CFPD about the incident, any CFPD police reports about the incident , any emails among between Wyatt or CFDFC and CFPD about the incident, and any records or reports from CF first responders about the incident".
Upon a review of the files maintained and possessed by the City of Central Falls, we have determined that the City does possess records that are responsive to your request.  Please see attached.
As you will see in the records, certain identifying information has been redacted pursuant to R.I. Gen. Laws § 38-2-2(4)(A)(I)(b), and the balancing test recognized in Direct Action for Rights and Equality v. Gannon, 713 A.2d 218 (R.I. 1998).  The nature of the redactions should be self-evident, but are the following: personally identifiable information, the day and month of birth, telephone numbers, employer information and ID numbers. In addition to guarding against identity theft, the relevant privacy interests outweigh the public's right to access this information.
The decision of this Department can be appealed pursuant to R.I. Gen. Laws § 38-2-8 by appealing this decision to the Mayor of the City of Central Falls, Maria Rivera, City of Central Falls, 580 Broad Street, Central Falls, RI 02863.  You may also file a complaint with the Department of Attorney General, 150 South Main Street, Providence, RI 02903, or the Providence County Superior Court.
Thank you for your time and cooperation.
Very truly yours,
Lisa Dias,
APRA Coordinator
Enc.

title: WA LexisNexis Master Contract - Participating Agencies

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: WA LexisNexis Master Contract - Participating Agencies
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. All records identifying government entities, agencies, municipalities, or authorized purchasers that have purchased goods or services through WA State Master Contract No. 00220 (LexisNexis Risk Solutions FL Inc., Category 3: Nationwide Public Records Search), including any Master Contract Sales Reports submitted by LexisNexis Risk Solutions FL Inc. to Enterprise Services.

2. Any purchase orders, participating addenda, or order forms issued under Master Contract No. 00220 by law enforcement agencies, sheriff's offices, police departments, or criminal justice agencies, for the period July 1, 2022 through the date this request is processed.

3. The pricing exhibit (Exhibit B) and any amendments to the LexisNexis Risk Solutions FL Inc. portion of Master Contract No. 00220.

Context: We are looking for Washington State agencies that are contributing data to LexisNexis's PSDEX (public safety data exchange) or AVCC (Accurint virtual crime center).

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Shawn Segal

communications.subject: RE: Washington Public Records Act Request: WA LexisNexis Master Contract - Participating Agencies
communications.communication:
DES PRR 26-0368,

This is an acknowledgment of your public records request below. On this website Contract Summary<https://apps.des.wa.gov/DESContracts/Home/ContractSummary/00220> you will find pricing exhibits, and contracts/amendments for Lexis Nexis. You can find this information as you scroll down under “Awarded Vendors” or a little bit further down there is a drop-down menu titled “Current Documents” that has these records as well. And on this website Statewide Contract (Master Contract) Sales Data by Customer, Contract, Vendor | Data.WA | State of Washington<https://data.wa.gov/Procurements-and-Contracts/Statewide-Contract-Master-Contract-Sales-Data-by-C/n8q6-4twj/data_preview>, you can find purchase and sales data. In the top right corner, there is a search bar where you can search for “00220” or “Employment income” and see the sales reported data and who purchased for this contract. There is a screen shot below showing this search as an example. It is important to use the “” symbols when typing in the contract number in the search to get an accurate search.

DES would not have purchase orders, participating addenda, or order forms as those would come from the departments that have used the contract themselves.

With this response your request is now closed, and this is our final response. DES does not intend to further address your public records request. The PRA’s one year statue of limitations to seek judicial review has started to run as of date of this communication. DES encourages you to ask any follow-up questions regarding our response. DES encourages you to notify DES of any concerns you may have about the adequacy of our response, compliance with the PRA or general concerns about any records we have provided. Please ask any question on or before May 26, 2026.

[cid:image002.png@01DCE6BC.F1617B50]

[Washington State Department of Enterprise Services logo]
Chris Tussing
Information Governance Coordinator
WAPRO Certified Public Records Officer
Office of Risk Management
Washington State Department of Enterprise Services
360-701-7405 | chris.tussing@des.wa.gov<mailto:chris.tussing@des.wa.gov>
DES.wa.gov<https://des.wa.gov/> | Capitol.wa.gov<https://capitol.wa.gov/> | EAP.wa.gov<https://eap.wa.gov/>

title: Flock Raven (San Mateo County Sheriff’s Office)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Flock Raven (San Mateo County Sheriff’s Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Flock Raven records created by the department and Flock Safety
Databases, spreadsheets, and any other documents that provide the following information collectively or individually:
- Number of alerts
- Description, details about the alert (Examples: gunfire, car crash, distress, sideshow)
- List of alert types (Examples: gunfire, car crash, etc)
- Maps and alert locations
- Raven locations
- Response times
- False positives, false negatives, and any record about the technology's accuracy
- Audo recordings of alerts
- Access logs, reason for searches or alerts, audits

Also search documents:
Promotional materials, user guides, and any PowerPoint presentations related to Flock Raven
Contracts and MOU's related to Flock Raven

Policies around Raven and other audio detection technologies
List of agencies your department shares access with and agencies sharing their Raven's with your department 

Please search January 2023 to today's date.
Release files and documents in their original formats in a rolling production.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Joey Scott

communications.subject: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request (Clare County)
communications.communication:
Dear Open Records FOIA Coordinator,

Pursuant to Michigan's Freedom of Information Act, MCL 15.231 et seq., I am requesting a copy of the Open Records county-wide assessment roll in its existing electronic format (CSV, DBF, or equivalent tabular export). If a single county-wide file does not exist, individual municipal-level exports that together cover all parcels in the county will satisfy this request.

Requested fields include, to the extent they exist in the record: parcel number, property address, owner name and mailing address, assessed value, state equalized value, taxable value, property classification, homestead/PRE status, school district, and any recorded sale date and sale price. I understand that many counties routinely provide similar datasets to commercial data aggregators such as Zillow and other real estate platforms, and I am requesting the same or comparable data.

I ask that this request be interpreted liberally, consistent with MCL 15.231(2). To the extent additional non-exempt fields exist in the record beyond those listed above, please include them. If the records are available for multiple years or assessment cycles, please include as broad a duration as is readily available rather than limiting the response to the current year alone. Where any question arises about whether to include or exclude non-exempt information, I respectfully ask that you err on the side of inclusion.

I am not requesting GIS data, parcel geometry, or any record that must be created — only a copy of existing electronic records. Please note that MCL 15.443(4) provides that the Enhanced Access to Public Records Act does not limit inspection and copying of public records under FOIA.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you.
Alexander Munro
Ypsilanti, MI
734-272-6612
awmunro@gmail.com

communications.subject: Re: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request (Clare County)
communications.communication:
Dear Mr. Munro,

Your FOIA request for a copy of the county-wide assessment roll in its existing electronic format outlined above is granted in its entirety. As stated in the email thread, these records will be provided to you upon receipt of requested payment by the Clare County Equalization Department.

1996 Public Act 462 in Michigan, known as the Enhanced Access to Public Records Act<https://www.google.com/search?q=Enhanced+Access+to+Public+Records+Act&rlz=1C1GCEA_enUS1185US1185&oq=1996+pa+462&gs_lcrp=EgZjaHJvbWUyBggAEEUYOTIHCAEQIRigATIHCAIQIRigATIHCAMQIRigATIHCAQQIRigATIHCAUQIRiPAtIBCDU0NzFqMGo3qAIAsAIA&sourceid=chrome&ie=UTF-8&mstk=AUtExfBl88uBeyD03kgRiQiOCYKGBMKFUJob1H_B05YeGTs0x2_x6LCciplEFVsC3hmFw46NLJc_ZM_0ffD0hJU5NbDyZiZXjID5PG0KxjtqXK_dTr33AZ9FWj_3PCtAFVTX-5CP4sneVr9007bAb-4fyyPB-pLuH09FQYzrem8BCrj6QSdKXcwMwtdjwnGIoeDzMDRwBMGDK2SPl9_aVOh_uioo-bQIpqDgcBNtoA1LgRxWrvo6Rbqrw_rtohGN-kO9ZBSYUkMRPKxi8t8bHtabu7MvrK3nQ7Ou95MIjA75gtGEIRtnBXKLIGcFNl82TNX_sazKmSE3F2vSvklK7jDmlHyaZari295ZzYeuJBK7zg4lVKykwVJuCeBKgrYFFcxHkgHPIMZvdfacJcoKF5jWeA&csui=3&ved=2ahUKEwi0v-Kmuf-TAxXdvisGHRDML4UQgK4QegQIARAB>, authorizes public bodies to provide enhanced, electronic access to public records and establish fees for this service, including for geographic information systems (GIS). It allows for fees beyond the cost of freedom of information act (FOIA) requests for immediate or electronic access.
Best regards,

Joy Bringold
Clare County Finance Coordinator, Accounts Payable
Clare County FOIA Coordinator
(989) 539-2510
bringoldj@clareco.net

communications.subject: RE: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request (Clare County)
communications.communication:
Ah, I'm sorry Joy,

I had to look back at our correspondence, I see the quote now for $1,100 -- pardon missing that in last email, you can disregard.

I do appreciate the prompt response nonetheless, and for granting my FOIA request "in its entirety." Because the $1,100 fee condition and your statement of the County's EAPRA-based position functionally denies the request as I read it, please treat this message as a written appeal under MCL 15.240.

Four grounds for the appeal.

1. I believe the County's reliance on EAPRA doesn't clear the MCL 15.234(10) carve-out. My understanding is that MCL 15.234(10) applies where the fee is "otherwise specifically provided by an act or statute." I believe the Michigan Court of Appeals construed "specific" to mean "explicit" in Grebner v. Clinton Charter Twp., 216 Mich. App. 736 (1996):

"A primary definition of the word 'specific' is 'explicit.' … In contrast, the Michigan Election Law, § 522, provides only for the payment of costs of preparing copies of voter registration records, as opposed to their sale. Clearly, this is not explicit authorization of the sale of voter registration rolls."

216 Mich. App. at 743. I believe EAPRA (MCL 15.441–15.446) does not explicitly authorize the sale of assessment rolls, and I believe it does not set a specific fee — it permits counties to adopt enhanced-access programs and charge a "reasonable" fee set by local resolution. On my reading of Grebner, I believe that isn't enough to trigger the 15.234(10) carve-out, and I believe the $1,100 figure is a departmental rate rather than one specifically provided by any act or statute.

2. I believe EAPRA's own savings clause forecloses the County's position. MCL 15.443(4) states: "This act does not limit the right of a person to inspect or copy a public record under [FOIA]." I believe the Senate Fiscal Agency's committee summary for the enacting bill (HB 5832, PA 462 of 1996) confirms the same reading:

"The bill would not limit access to a public record under the FOIA. A public record available by enhanced access would have to be made available for inspection or copying in accordance with [FOIA]."

SFA Committee Summary HB 5832 (H-3), 9-18-96, at 2. (Attached.)

I believe the position in your April 23 letter — that EAPRA "allows for fees beyond the cost of freedom of information act (FOIA) requests for immediate or electronic access" — is precisely the kind of EAPRA-over-FOIA displacement I believe MCL 15.443(4) and the SFA analysis were drafted to prevent.

3. I believe the FOIA exemption list tells against Clare's position. MCL 15.243 enumerates FOIA's exemptions, and I believe assessment-roll data is not among them. FOIA (1976 PA 442) predates EAPRA (1996 PA 462) by about 20 years. I believe that if the Legislature had intended EAPRA to make assessment records FOIA-exempt, the straightforward move would have been to amend MCL 15.243 to list them — rather than authorize a fee structure that would indirectly accomplish the same outcome.

4. I believe EAPRA's "recover over time" window has long since run. I believe EAPRA's reasonable-fee standard is "a charge calculated to enable a public body to recover over time only those operating expenses directly related to its provision of enhanced access." (SFA Summary at 1.) EAPRA took effect December 26, 1996 — nearly 30 years ago. I believe that under any reasonable amortization horizon for a 1990s-era enhanced-access system investment, the County's recovery clock has already run. I believe that if the County has continued collecting fees beyond that recovery, those fees are no longer "reasonable" under EAPRA's own framework. And I believe that if the County is reinvesting fee revenue into new enhanced-access capabilities and re-starting the recovery clock indefinitely, EAPRA does not authorize that either.

If the $1,100 is tied to a specific enhanced-access system investment, I believe the County should be in a position to identify what that investment was, roughly what has been collected against it since 1996, and when full recovery was projected. They may even find those costs to have already been amortized many times.

Requested relief. Pursuant to MCL 15.240(2), I respectfully ask that:

The denial (by way of the $1,100 fee condition) be reversed; and
Clare County produce the existing electronic assessment roll at FOIA cost itemized under MCL 15.234, on the County's cost itemization form.
If this appeal is denied in whole or in part, I reserve all rights under MCL 15.240(1)(b), including a civil action in Clare County Circuit Court with fee-shifting under MCL 15.240(6) and punitive damages under MCL 15.240(7) if the denial is found arbitrary and capricious. Should this take a month?

Happy to discuss any of this by phone or email if that's easier.

Thanks,
Alexander Munro
awmunro@gmail.com · 734-272-6612
34 N Huron St OFC | Ypsilanti, MI | 48197

communications.subject: Re: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request (Clare County)
communications.communication:
Dear Mr. Munro,

We have received your correspondence dated 4/27/2026, which the County is treating as a written appeal under the Michigan Freedom of Information Act (FOIA), MCL 15.240, challenging the fee estimate associated with your request for the County's electronic assessment roll.

After review, the County denies your appeal and upholds the fee estimate of $1,100.

The requested record is maintained and made available through the County's enhanced access system.  The County has adopted this system pursuant to the Enhanced Access to Public Records Act (EAPRA), which authorizes a public body to provide public records through electronic means and to charge a reasonable fee for that access as established by the public body.


  1.
Applicability of MCL 15.234(10)
FOIA provides that its fee provisions do not apply where "the fee for a public record is otherwise specifically provided by an act or statute." See MCL 15.234.  The County has established, pursuant to EAPRA, a fee schedule governing access to electronic assessment data.  The County interprets EAPRA as authorizing such fees independent of FOIA's cost-recovery limitations.  Accordingly, Clare County concludes that MCL 15.234(10) applies, and the FOIA fee limitations you cite do not control the fee for this record as provided through the enhanced access system
  2.
Consistency with EAPRA
The County acknowledges the savings clause in MCL 15.443(4).  However, the County does not interpret that provision to prohibit a public body from accessing an EARPRA-authorized fee when a requester seeks a record in the form and manner provided by the enhanced access system.  The requested electronic assessment roll is a product of that system and is not maintained as a separate, preexisting record outside of it in the format requested.  As such, the County considers the EAPRA fee applicable to the request submitted.
  3.
Nature of the Fee
The $1,100 fee reflects the County's adopted charge for providing the electronic assessment dataset through its enhanced access system.  The fee is not based on FOIA labor or copying components under MCL 15.234, but rather on the County's EAPRA -authorized fee structure
  4.
Other Arguments Raised
The County has considered your arguments regarding statutory interpretation, including your reliance on Grebner v. Clinton Charter Twp., 216 Mich App 736 (1996), and your discussion of legislative history and amortization.  The County respectfully disagrees with your conclusions and maintains that its fee structure is authorized under EAPRA and applicable to this request.

This correspondence constitutes the County's final determination on your appeal.  You have the right to commence a civil action in the Clare County Circuit Court under MCL 15.240.

If you wish to proceed with your request, the County will process it upon receipt of payment of the $1,100 fee.

If you have any questions, please contact Joy Bringold, FOIA Coordinator cc'd on this correspondence.

Sincerely,



Lori Phelps, Administrator

Clare County

225 W. Main St

Harrison, MI 48625

989-539-2510 ext. 0 (w)

989-418-9679

phelpsl@clareco.net<mailto:phelpsl@clareco.net>

Book time with Lori Phelps <https://outlook.office.com/bookwithme/user/7220c02650d94afcbb99480933a8b20b@clareco.net?anonymous&ismsaljsauthenabled&ep=pcard>

communications.communication:
Hello Joy and Lori,

Pursuant to Michigan's Freedom of Information Act, MCL 15.231 et seq., I am submitting a new and distinct request. Please consider this request separate from my earlier request for the Clare County's assessment roll (which was denied on appeal April 30, 2026); as this concerns a different category of records.

I am requesting copies of the following records, to the extent they exist, for the period January 1, 2020 through the present:

1) Data-sharing or licensing agreements between Clare County and any of the following entities, or their subsidiaries: Zillow Group, Redfin, Move Inc. / Realtor.com, CoreLogic, ATTOM Data Solutions, Black Knight / ICE Mortgage Technology, First American Financial, Old Republic, Stewart Title, Fidelity National Financial, LexisNexis Risk Solutions, DataTree, PropertyRadar, NETROnline, Constellation Software / BS&A, Michigan REALTORS, and any multiple listing services (MiRealSource, RealComp, etc.). If no such records exist, please confirm in writing.

2) Email correspondence between Clare County employees (in the Equalization, Treasurer, Information Systems, or Administrator's offices) and representatives of the entities listed in #1, concerning provision of assessment-roll data, parcel data, sales data, or any related real estate dataset. If no such records exist, please confirm in writing.

3) Invoices issued by Clare County to any of the entities listed in #1, for assessment data, parcel data, sales data, enhanced-access subscriptions, or any related dataset. If no such records exist, please confirm in writing.

4) Records of payments received by Clare County from any of the entities listed in #1. If no such records exist, please confirm in writing.

5) Transmittal records (cover emails, ShareFile transfer logs, FTP logs, mailing receipts, etc.) showing what assessment-roll, parcel, or sales data files were transmitted by Clare County to any of the entities listed in #1. If no such records exist, please confirm in writing.

6) The County's current code of ethics policy, if such a policy exists. If such a policy does not exist, please confirm in writing.

Clare County's BoC-approved enhanced access fee schedule(s) in effect at any point during the request period, and any amendments thereto. I understand from Lori's explanation that Clare County's position is that they get to charge whatever they arbitrarily decide to charge; I would like to see a copy of what Clare's BoC decided to charge. Please also include the meeting agenda and minutes from the Board of Commissioner's authorization.

I am not requesting any record that must be created. I am not requesting GIS data, parcel geometry, or any sensitive personnel information. I ask that the request be interpreted liberally consistent with MCL 15.231(2).

Thank you for your help with this!

Alex Munro
34 N Huron St, OFC
Ypsilanti, MI 48197
awmunro@gmail.com
734-272-6612

title: Disciplinary and Activity Records: Full-Time Officers (2024-2026)
tags: public records audit: officer personnel and conduct files (2024-2026)

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: Disciplinary and Activity Records: Full-Time Officers (2024-2026)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (5 ILCS 140), I hereby request the following records for the period of January 1, 2024, through April 1, 2026, regarding all full-time sworn officers currently employed by your department:
Disciplinary Records: Any internal affairs investigations, citizen complaints, or disciplinary actions, including the nature, status, and final outcome of each.

Use of Force: All Use of Force reports (also known as Tactical Response Reports) involving these officers.

Personnel Files: The complete personnel files for these officers, including but not limited to: hire dates, training certificates, commendations, and performance evaluations.

Exemption Note: Per 5 ILCS 140/7(1)(c), the disclosure of information that bears on the public duties of public employees shall not be considered an invasion of personal privacy. Please redact only private information (SSNs, home addresses, personal phone numbers) as required by law while releasing all records related to their professional conduct and training.

Format & Fees
I request that these records be provided in PDF format via MuckRock's electronic delivery system. As a non-commercial requester:

The first 50 pages of any scanned records are free (5 ILCS 140/6).

Search and redaction time may not be charged (5 ILCS 140/6(b)).

I look forward to your response within five (5) business days.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter.

Sincerely,

Illinois Transparency Project

---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: <208657-14224079@requests.muckrock.com<mailto:208657-14224079@requests.muckrock.com>>
Date: Mon, Apr 13, 2026 at 8:06 AM
Subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: Disciplinary and Activity Records: Full-Time Officers (2024-2026)
To: <polopd@poloil.gov<mailto:polopd@poloil.gov>>


Polo Police Department
FOIA Office
116 S. Franklin Ave.
Polo, IL 61064

April 13, 2026

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (5 ILCS 140), I hereby request the following records for the period of January 1, 2024, through April 1, 2026, regarding all full-time sworn officers currently employed by your department:
Disciplinary Records: Any internal affairs investigations, citizen complaints, or disciplinary actions, including the nature, status, and final outcome of each.

Use of Force: All Use of Force reports (also known as Tactical Response Reports) involving these officers.

Personnel Files: The complete personnel files for these officers, including but not limited to: hire dates, training certificates, commendations, and performance evaluations.

Exemption Note: Per 5 ILCS 140/7(1)(c), the disclosure of information that bears on the public duties of public employees shall not be considered an invasion of personal privacy. Please redact only private information (SSNs, home addresses, personal phone numbers) as required by law while releasing all records related to their professional conduct and training.

Format & Fees
I request that these records be provided in PDF format via MuckRock's electronic delivery system. As a non-commercial requester:

The first 50 pages of any scanned records are free (5 ILCS 140/6).

Search and redaction time may not be charged (5 ILCS 140/6(b)).

I look forward to your response within five (5) business days.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter.

Sincerely,

Illinois Transparency Project

View request history, upload responsive documents, and report problems here:
https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2262885/

Filed via MuckRock.com
E-mail (Preferred): 208657-14224079@requests.muckrock.com<mailto:208657-14224079@requests.muckrock.com>

PLEASE NOTE OUR NEW ADDRESS
For mailed responses, please address (see note):
MuckRock News
DEPT MR 208657
263 Huntington Ave
Boston, MA 02115

PLEASE NOTE: This request is not filed by a MuckRock staff member, but is being sent through MuckRock by the above in order to better track, share, and manage public records requests. Also note that improperly addressed (i.e., with the requester's name rather than "MuckRock News" and the department number) requests might be returned as undeliverable.

communications.subject: RE: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: Disciplinary and Activity Records: Full-Time Officers (2024-2026)
communications.communication:
Attorney Suits,

The Illinois Transparency Project finds the City’s response legally deficient.

Unauthorized Scope Restriction: The request for "all full-time sworn officers" for the 2024–2026 period includes all individuals who served in that capacity during that timeframe. Transparency regarding police conduct is not contingent on an officer’s current employment status.

Independent Record Categories: The absence of "Disciplinary Records" does not render "Personnel Files" or "Use of Force" reports inapplicable. These are distinct categories of public records. Personnel files and Use of Force reports must be produced regardless of whether a disciplinary action was taken.

Willful Withholding: The City has previously acknowledged the existence of "Law Enforcement Personnel Files" and specific "employment history" summaries for officers active during this period. Denying their applicability in this forum is a material misrepresentation.

We are granting the City five (5) business days to provide a complete and compliant response. Failure to do so will result in an immediate Request for Review with the Illinois Attorney General’s Public Access Counselor (PAC) to seek a binding opinion and potential civil penalties for willful non-compliance.

communications.communication:
Attorney Suits,

The Illinois Transparency Project acknowledges your most recent correspondence. However, we must point out a significant inconsistency in the City’s representations:

Admission of Records: In your email dated April 27, 2026, you stated you were "ready to forward the response" but needed additional time to "notify the employee and his or her representative." This was an explicit admission that responsive records for a specific individual exist and were prepared for disclosure.

Attempted Recantation: Your subsequent message attempting to exclude records based on "current employment" status contradicts your April 27 statement. As previously clarified, the request covers all officers active during the 2024–2026 timeframe.

Final Deadline: The City has already exceeded the statutory deadlines provided by 5 ILCS 140. We will not grant any further extensions beyond the Friday, May 1, 2026 date you proposed.

If the full production, including the Personnel Files and Use of Force reports for all officers (current and former) from the 2024–2026 period, is not uploaded to this portal by 5:00 PM on Friday, the Project will immediately file a Request for Review with the Illinois Attorney General’s Public Access Counselor and will refer the matter of these conflicting statements to the appropriate disciplinary authorities.

We expect full compliance without further "miscommunication."

Sincerely,

Illinois Transparency Project

communications.subject: RE: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: Disciplinary and Activity Records: Full-Time Officers (2024-2026)
communications.communication:
Attorney Suits,

The Illinois Transparency Project confirms receipt of the partial production containing records for Sergeant Rockwood and Officer Montanez. We note your intent to provide the files for Sergeant Pilgrim and Chief Coppotelli tomorrow morning.

However, your statement that "There are four full-time sworn officers with the Polo Police Department" appears to reference only the City’s current active roster.

Please be reminded that this public records request is an audit of the timeframe between January 1, 2024, and April 1, 2026. The scope explicitly covers all individuals who served as full-time sworn officers at any point during this 27-month period, which inherently includes any officers who resigned, retired, or were separated during that time.

Providing records for only the four officers who are currently employed today does not satisfy the requirements of the request.

To achieve full statutory compliance by your Friday deadline, please ensure that tomorrow morning's final production includes the Personnel Files and Use of Force reports for all former officers who served on the roster during the 2024–2026 window. We will review the complete historical production tomorrow morning.

Sincerely,

Illinois Transparency Project

communications.subject: RE: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: Disciplinary and Activity Records: Full-Time Officers (2024-2026)
communications.communication:
Attorney Suits,

The Illinois Transparency Project acknowledges receipt of the redacted file for Sergeant Pilgrim.

However, we categorically reject your attempt to improperly narrow the scope of this public records audit through a bad-faith interpretation of the original request. The initial communication established a historical audit period of January 1, 2024, through April 1, 2026. The Project formally clarified this scope in writing yesterday, stating the request encompasses "all individuals who served as full-time sworn officers at any point during this 27-month period, which inherently includes any officers who resigned, retired, or were separated."

A historical audit of 2024–2026 is mathematically impossible if the City only produces records for individuals employed on the arbitrary date of April 12, 2026.

Furthermore, a forensic cross-reference of the City of Polo’s public municipal salary records for the years 2021 through 2025 reveals a comprehensive roster of City personnel. Our database pull indicates the following individuals received municipal salaries during recent operational years:

Timothy Rockwood, Matthew Coppotelli, Michael Mumford, Geraud Zinsou, Troy Randall, Kendall Kyker, Sydney Bartelt, Kevin Becerra, Cale Fry, Nathan Millard, Rodney Lucas, Dakota Meyer, Susan Waszak, Jamie Nielsen, Kaden Coppotelli, Brexton Flynn, Dennis Christen, Vernon Shetler, David Pilgrim, Brenden Kane, Justin Grobe.

By comparing this public payroll data against your current production, it is clear the City is unlawfully withholding the personnel files of multiple former sworn officers who fall squarely within the requested 2024–2026 timeframe. The omitted officers include, but are not limited to:

Kaden Coppotelli

Rodney Lucas

Dakota Meyer

Kevin Becerra

Geraud Zinsou

Additionally, regarding your statement that Chief Coppotelli's file is "too large" for your email server: Technical transmission limitations do not toll statutory FOIA deadlines. It is the City's legal responsibility to utilize standard file-sharing solutions (e.g., secure cloud links, Dropbox, or the MuckRock direct upload portal) to fulfill its obligations.

To answer your concluding question regarding whether you have "omitted anything": Yes, the City has willfully omitted the records of the aforementioned officers and has failed to deliver the Chief's file. The City has exhausted all statutory extensions. The City has until 5:00 PM today (Saturday, May 2, 2026) to cure this deficiency by successfully uploading the file for Chief Coppotelli AND the complete files for all former officers.

If all required files are not received by 5:00 PM today, the Project will consider your response a final, willful denial. We will not entertain further debate, and we will not grant extensions for technical difficulties. At 5:01 PM, we will immediately file a Request for Review with the Attorney General’s Public Access Counselor (PAC) for willful obstruction, submitting your correspondence and the City's public salary data as evidence of bad-faith concealment.

We await the remainder of the legally required production by 5:00 PM.

Sincerely,

Illinois Transparency Project

title: Dunwoody Suitet 239 Access Logs  February 12th 2026

communications.subject: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Dunwoody Suitet 239 Access Logs  February 12th 2026
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All electronic access logs, keycard entry logs, badge reader logs, or any other records generated by the access control system at 4800 Ashford Dunwoody Rd NE, Suite 239, Dunwoody, GA 30338 (Dunwoody Police Department lobby) on February 12, 2026, between 8:00am and 12:00pm Eastern Time.

This request includes all records showing any individual who entered or attempted to enter Suite 239 during that time window, including but not limited to the identity of the individual, the time of entry, and the credential used to gain access.

For purposes of this request, "records" includes any electronically stored data generated by the access control system, whether maintained by the City of Dunwoody directly or by a third-party vendor on the city's behalf.

Pursuant to O.C.G.A. § 50-18-71(b)(1), I request that responsive records be provided in their native electronic format with full metadata preserved, including but not limited to timestamps and user identification data.

If any portion of this request is denied, I request that you:
(1) Identify each responsive record being withheld with sufficient detail to permit me to assess the basis for withholding;
(2) Cite the specific statutory exemption(s) claimed for each record or portion thereof; and
(3) Pursuant to the segregability requirement of O.C.G.A. § 50-18-72(a)(44), produce all non-exempt portions of partially exempt records with redactions clearly marked and justified on a field-by-field basis.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jason Hunyar
