title: Colorado Information Sharing Consortium (CISC) - Data Transfer to LexisNexis

communications.subject: Colorado Open Records Act Request: Colorado Information Sharing Consortium (CISC) - Data Transfer to LexisNexis
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Colorado Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1) Technical Specifications: Any Interface Control Document (ICD) or technical implementation guide that defines the data structure and data elements for the automated export of Records Management System (RMS) or Computer-Aided Dispatch (CAD) data to the "CISC Data Warehouse" or the LexisNexis "PSDEX private container".
2) Data Mapping: Any "Data Translation" guides, field-mapping tables, or schemas used to configure the transfer of local agency data into the LexisNexis Public Safety Data Exchange (PSDEX) or Virtual Crime Center (VCC) platforms.
3) Field Lists: Any record or "Schedule A" line item defining the "Database Interface" capabilities, specifically identifying which categories of information (e.g., Victim PII, Witness Contact Data, Real-Time Jail Bookings) are included in the nightly (or regular) automated feed.

Exclusion Note: We are not seeking URLs, passwords, network IP addresses, encryption keys, or sensitive security information, or contents of any data fields. We are seeking only the field name of the data elements (e.g., "Name," "DOB," "SSN," "Narrative") that have been approved for mass transfer to LexisNexis/PSDEX.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Alan J. Segal

communications.subject: Re: Colorado Open Records Act Request: Colorado Information Sharing Consortium (CISC) - Data Transfer to LexisNexis
communications.communication:
Dear Alan J. Segal,

Thank you for your Colorado Open Records Act (CORA) request. After reviewing the items you listed, I want to clarify the role of the Colorado Information Sharing Consortium (CISC) in relation to the systems and data connections you referenced.

CISC does not create, host, or manage any technical data interfaces, data feeds, or system‑to‑system connections for RMS or CAD exports. All data connections, data translation processes, interface specifications, and technical documentation associated with the Public Safety Data Exchange (PSDEX), the Accurint Virtual Crime Center (AVCC), or the “CISC Data Warehouse” environment are developed, maintained, and administered solely by our contracted vendor, LexisNexis.

Because of this structure:

  *   CISC does not possess Interface Control Documents (ICDs), data‑mapping guides, field‑translation tables, or technical specifications for RMS/CAD exports.
  *   CISC does not configure, review, or approve the specific data elements that any individual agency elects to share with LexisNexis.
  *   Any “Schedule A,” field list, or definition of what data elements an agency provides is an agreement directly between that agency and LexisNexis, not CISC.
  *   CISC does not receive, store, or maintain copies of those documents and therefore has no responsive records to provide under CORA.

If you are seeking the technical specifications or field‑level documentation for the PSDEX/VCC data interfaces, you may wish to request those materials directly from LexisNexis or from the individual agencies that have executed data‑sharing agreements with them.

Sincerely,


Robert Nanney

Executive Director

720-397-9471

[Logo of the Colorado Information Sharing Consortium.]

https://cisc.colorado.gov<https://cisc.colorado.gov/>

communications.subject: RE: Colorado Open Records Act Request: Colorado Information Sharing Consortium (CISC) - Data Transfer to LexisNexis
communications.communication:
Hi Mr. Nanney, 

Thank you for the detailed response, I appreciate the information. 

A few quick clarifications:
1) You mentioned asking for the details from LexisNexis directly - that seems like the most efficient way to answer my questions and wouldn't require member agencies to deal with CORA requests. Are you able to provide contact info for an account manager at LexisNexis that may be able to provide the information I requested?
2) I see 120 CISC member agencies listed on the website. You mentioned reaching out to them directly if needed. Do they all have the data sharing agreements or just a subset? If it's just a subset, do you mind letting me know which ones? I don't want to waste anyone's time with a request for documents they don't have. 
3) As I was learning more about your agency I noticed the most recent meeting minutes available were from Sept. 2024. Is there any way to view more recent meeting minutes? 

Thank you again for the help! Have a great week. 

Best,
Alan

communications.subject: Re: Colorado Open Records Act Request: Colorado Information Sharing Consortium (CISC) - Data Transfer to LexisNexis
communications.communication:
Dear Alan J. Segal,



Thank you for your follow-up.



1. LexisNexis / vendor contact

CISC does not maintain a designated LexisNexis contact for purposes of responding to public records requests. As noted previously, the technical materials you are seeking (e.g., interface specifications, field mappings, or data schemas) are created and maintained by LexisNexis or by individual member agencies in connection with their implementations. You may wish to direct those requests to the individual agencies. We do not maintain a list of each agency’s CORA contact person.



2. Member agency participation and agreements

CISC has a master agreement with LexisNexis. Member agencies that elect to participate do so by executing a joinder (referred to as an addendum and joinder). By executing that document, a member agency becomes a party to the master agreement as a “Customer.” We are providing a copy of the master agreement with LexisNexis, along with the standard joinder form, for your reference.



3. “Schedule A” (what it is and what it is not)

We are also providing a redacted copy of the applicable Schedule A to the agreement. This was redacted by LexisNexis to remove their confidential commercial information as permitted under CORA.



To clarify terminology:

  *   Schedule A in this agreement is a service and pricing schedule that sets forth the LexisNexis products, features, and subscription terms provided under the contract.
  *
It contains items such as subscription fees, product features, and related terms and conditions.
  *
It does not identify or define the specific data elements that any CISC member agency contributes to or shares through the system.

Under the agreement structure, Schedule A governs the services being provided, while decisions regarding what data is contributed or shared are made by individual member agencies.

4. Data contributions and control
By way of clarification:

  *   Each participating agency contributes its own law enforcement data to the system (referred to as “Customer Data Contributions”).
  *
That data is stored in the CISC Regional Data Warehouse (a PSDEX private container maintained by LexisNexis).
  *
Each agency is responsible for the content of its data and retains ownership of that data.

Importantly, data-sharing is controlled at the agency level:

  *   By default, data contributed by a member agency is shared within the CISC consortium.
  *
Sharing beyond the consortium (e.g., with other law enforcement agencies outside CISC or other third parties) occurs only if and to the extent the contributing agency affirmatively authorizes that sharing.
  *
Agencies may tailor, limit, or revoke those permissions.

CISC does not determine what data an agency contributes, does not control what data elements are included in any feed, and does not maintain agency-specific field lists, mappings, or similar technical documentation.

5. Board meeting minutes
CISC maintains board meeting minutes and will provide copies of all minutes in our possession responsive to your request. For convenience, some minutes are also posted on the CISC website, although that posting may not always reflect the most current set.

Please let me know if you have any additional questions.

Sincerely,


Robert Nanney

Executive Director

720-397-9471

[Logo of the Colorado Information Sharing Consortium.]

https://cisc.colorado.gov<https://cisc.colorado.gov/>

title: Public Records Request: Darkowl

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Public Records Request: Darkowl
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

- All contracts, licenses, MOUs, MOAs, data agreements, letters of intent, State of Work documents, Scope of Work documents, and other agreement documents with Darkowl that were active any time between 1/1/2021 and 3/25/2026.
- All financial documents or records of financial transactions, exchanges or obligations related to Darkowl. Responsive materials include all invoices, bills, receipts, check stubs, or any other records, including those possessed in an electronic format.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

e
